articles.topic: daily multivitamin biological aging epigenetic clocks
articles.headline: Your Daily Multivitamin Might Be Buying You Extra Time
articles.body: Researchers at Harvard and Mass General Brigham ran a rigorous two-year randomized clinical trial on 958 older adults and found something genuinely surprising. People who took a daily multivitamin showed slower biological aging across five DNA-based epigenetic clocks, which estimate how fast your body is aging at the cellular level. The effect translated to roughly four months less biological aging over two years. People who were already biologically older than their actual age got the biggest benefit. Cocoa extract, tested alongside the multivitamin, showed no effect on aging clocks.
articles.takeaway: A basic daily multivitamin may offer a modest but real aging benefit, especially if your diet has gaps. It's not a magic pill, but the evidence from a gold-standard clinical trial is encouraging.

product.name: Thorne Vitamin D3/K2
product.blurb: Most adults are deficient. D3 with K2 is the combination that actually works.

articles.topic: extra virgin olive oil gut bacteria cognitive function
articles.headline: Not All Olive Oil Is the Same. Your Brain Notices the Difference.
articles.body: A two-year study tracking 656 adults aged 55 to 75 found that people who used virgin olive oil instead of refined olive oil showed better cognitive performance and more diverse gut bacteria. The key appears to be a specific gut bacterium called Adlercreutzia, which was significantly more abundant in virgin olive oil users and accounted for about half of the cognitive boost seen in memory and problem-solving tests. Refined olive oil users, by contrast, showed declining gut diversity over time. The study, published in the journal Microbiome, was the first to directly examine how olive oil type affects brain health through the gut.
articles.takeaway: Swap refined olive oil for extra virgin wherever you can. The polyphenols that make it more expensive are the same ones feeding the gut bacteria linked to sharper thinking.
articles.pexelsQuery: extra virgin olive oil bottle kitchen

articles.topic: semaglutide depression anxiety mental health reduction
articles.headline: Ozempic May Be Quietly Helping People's Mental Health Too
articles.body: A massive study following nearly 100,000 Swedish adults over 13 years found something unexpected about GLP-1 weight loss drugs like Ozempic. People taking semaglutide had a 42% lower risk of worsening mental illness during treatment periods compared to when they were off it. Depression risk dropped 44%, anxiety disorders fell 38%, and substance use hospitalizations dropped 47%. The study, published in The Lancet Psychiatry, looked at people who already had depression or anxiety diagnoses. Researchers believe semaglutide may be influencing brain pathways tied to mood and reward, not just appetite.
articles.takeaway: If you or someone you know is on GLP-1 medication, the mental health data is increasingly pointing in a positive direction. Worth a conversation with your doctor about the full picture.

articles.headline: Your Strength Starts Declining at 35. Here's the Good News.
articles.body: A 47-year study out of Sweden's Karolinska Institute tracked real people across their entire adult lives and found that fitness, strength, and muscle endurance begin slipping around age 35, with the decline picking up speed over time. That's the kind of news that lands differently when you're already in your 40s. But here's what the researchers also found: adults who became active later in life still made meaningful gains. It's not too late. The clock is ticking, but it hasn't stopped. What you do in the next few years of your 30s and 40s matters more than most people realize.
articles.takeaway: You don't need to reverse time. You just need to start building or protecting your strength now. Even picking it up later in life shows real results, so don't use your age as a reason to wait.

quote.text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
quote.author: Mark Twain
quote.context: American author and humorist whose writing on human nature remains deeply relevant to behavior change and self-improvement.

workout.title: 60-Second Surge: Speed Play for Busy Runners
workout.tip: If you are short on time, you can do just the warmup and 4 to 5 surges and still get a quality session in. Quality beats quantity on a Friday.
workout.warmup: Walk briskly for 2 minutes, then jog easy for 3 minutes at a pace where you can hold a full conversation. Shake out your arms and do 10 slow leg swings on each side before picking up the pace.

exercises.name: Easy Base Jog
exercises.notes: Run at an easy, conversational effort. This is your anchor pace for the whole session. Settle in and find your rhythm.

exercises.name: 60-Second Hard Surge
exercises.notes: Push to a comfortably hard effort on the surge, a 7 to 8 out of 10. Not a sprint, but faster than comfortable. Recover fully with the easy jog before the next one.

exercises.name: Easy Finish Jog
exercises.notes: Drop back to easy, conversational pace to close out the run. Breathe through your nose if you can. This is your flush.

workout.cooldown: Walk for 2 to 3 minutes until your heart rate settles, then do a standing quad stretch, a calf stretch against a wall, and one hip flexor lunge hold on each side for 20 seconds.

product.name: HOKA Clifton 9
product.blurb: Max cushion for easy days and long runs. Keeps your legs fresh for hard sessions.

meal.title: Seared Scallop and White Bean Bowl with Lemon Herb Dressing
ingredients: 300g large sea scallops (about 8 to 10 scallops), patted completely dry
1 can (400g) white cannellini beans, drained and rinsed
2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, divided
1 tablespoon fresh parsley or basil, chopped
Salt, black pepper, and a pinch of chili flakes
meal.instructions: 1. Heat a cast iron or stainless steel pan over high heat until very hot. Pat scallops dry with paper towel, season with salt and pepper. Add 1 tbsp olive oil to the pan, then sear scallops 90 seconds per side without touching them. Remove and rest. 2. In the same pan on medium heat, add remaining olive oil and garlic, cook 30 seconds until fragrant. Add white beans and cherry tomatoes and warm through for 2 minutes. Season with salt, pepper, and chili flakes. 3. Build the bowl: greens on the bottom, beans and tomatoes on top, scallops arranged over everything. 4. Drizzle with lemon juice, scatter fresh herbs and capers, finish with a light drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.
meal.tip: Dry scallops are non-negotiable. Any moisture on the surface and you steam them instead of sear them, and you lose the golden crust that makes this dish.

product.name: Greater Goods Digital Food Scale
product.blurb: Measure your food for two weeks and you will understand your body better than any diet book.
